title: Investigate and fix API key Authorization header format
description: Integration tests fail with 'invalid token' when using api_key auth. The current header is 'Authorization: token <key>' but Orpheus/Redacted may expect a different format (e.g. bare key or different prefix). Needs investigation against live API docs or trial-and-error during fixture capture.
notes: Root cause confirmed: API-key Authorization header is per-tracker. Orpheus wants 'token <key>' (Gazelle recommended); RED wants the bare key (no prefix). Fix: added api_key_prefix param to GazelleTransport (default 'token '), RedactedClient sets it to ''. Also discovered during live verification: (1) RED migrated redacted.ch -> redacted.sh (old domain returns 410); updated REDACTED_BASE_URL. (2) RED rejects requests lacking a custom User-Agent; added default UA 'python-gazelle'. (3) Orpheus omits userstats.requiredRatio -> made UserStats.required_ratio Optional. Orpheus auth VERIFIED live (user.me OK, id=REDACTED). RED still returns 401 'bad credentials' = key value issue (likely rotated key in .env), NOT a code bug; awaiting valid key to confirm.
close_reason: Fixed per-tracker API-key auth: Orpheus uses 'token <key>', RED uses bare key (api_key_prefix param). Also fixed RED domain redacted.ch->redacted.sh, added default User-Agent (RED 401s without one), made UserStats.required_ratio optional (Orpheus omits it). Both trackers verified live: Orpheus id=REDACTED, RED id=REDACTED.

title: Task 12: Integration tests

Integration tests for OrpheusClient and RedactedClient against live APIs (read-only, both auth methods). Finalize __init__.py public exports. Part of core-api-client plan, epic python-gazelle-ahf.
close_reason: Integration tests, public exports, and security fixes committed

title: Task 11: API fixtures and model tests

title: Task 8: Resource layer

title: [Epic] Core API Client
description: Foundation library for all Gazelle tracker interactions. Covers authentication, rate limiting, retry logic, full endpoint coverage (torrents, artists, requests, collages, user, inbox, notifications), typed response models, and tracker-specific adapters for Orpheus and Redacted. All other feature epics depend on this.
close_reason: Epic complete. All four feature areas verified and closed: 41c (auth/session), z0v (rate limit/retry), 59a (endpoint coverage), 11h (Orpheus/RED adapters). Implementation shipped via closed Task 1-12 issues + nmq/6ue; full non-integration suite 42 passed on main (4b85d0b). Downstream epics 7gj (Cross-Seed) and p53 (Upload Monitoring) depend on this but are separate, not-yet-built scope.

title: torrent: add edition/remaster fields + description + log detail
description:
Torrent model (action=torrent) drops edition/remaster info needed to distinguish editions. Add to Torrent (all Optional):
- remaster_year (remasterYear), remaster_title (remasterTitle), remaster_record_label (remasterRecordLabel), remaster_catalogue_number (remasterCatalogueNumber)
- description (release description)
- log_checksum (logChecksum), log_count (logCount), rip_log_ids (ripLogIds: list[int])
- free_reason (freeReason, Orpheus), reported (bool)
TDD against tests/fixtures/orpheus/torrent.json (real fixture). Tracker-divergence: RED may omit some (e.g. freeReason); keep Optional.
close_reason: Added edition/remaster fields to Torrent (remaster_year/title/record_label/catalogue_number), description, log detail (log_checksum, log_count, rip_log_ids), free_reason, reported — all Optional. log_checksum/log_count/free_reason Orpheus-only (RED omits -> None). TDD vs both fixtures. Reviewed clean. 103 tests pass, gate clean.

title: artist: enrich Artist with discography, statistics, similar_artists
description:
Artist model (action=artist) drops the most valuable parts of the response. Currently exposes only id/name/body/image/tags. Add:
- discography: the artist's torrent groups. NOTE different shape than action=torrentgroup — uses groupId/groupName/groupYear, tags as a {tagId: name} map, "torrent" (singular key) list with hasFile, extendedArtists. Needs a dedicated model (e.g. ArtistTorrentGroup), do NOT reuse models.torrents.TorrentGroup. Source key in response: "torrentgroup".
- statistics: {numGroups, numTorrents, numSeeders, numLeechers, numSnatches, numRequests} -> ArtistStatistics submodel.
- similar_artists: list of {artistId, name, score, similarId}.
Lower-priority same-endpoint extras (optional, can fold in): vanity_house, body_bbcode, has_bookmarked, notifications_enabled, requests.
All Optional/tolerant per tracker-divergence convention. TDD against tests/fixtures/orpheus/artist.json (real fixture exists). Ground truth: https://github.com/OPSnet/Gazelle/blob/master/docs/07-API.md
close_reason: Enriched Artist with statistics (ArtistStatistics), similar_artists (ArtistSimilar — distinct from SimilarArtist), discography (ArtistTorrentGroup/DiscographyTorrent/DiscographyArtist, alias 'torrentgroup'), plus vanity_house/body_bbcode. Dedicated discography models due to shape divergence (groupId/groupName, tags map, singular 'torrent', groupCategoryID casing). All Optional/tolerant. TDD vs orpheus/artist.json. Reviewed: one low-sev note (discography tags union unverified on RED) tracked under fixture-capture follow-up. 100 tests pass, gate clean.

title: torrents: add get_group() for torrentgroup action
description: Add TorrentResource.get_group(group_id) calling ajax.php action=torrentgroup, returning a TorrentGroup with all editions/torrents. Highest-value gap: complements existing torrents.get() (action=torrent), which only returns a single torrent with the group embedded. Add/extend TorrentGroup model + fixtures for both trackers.
close_reason: Implemented TorrentResource.get_group() for action=torrentgroup; enriched TorrentGroup (editions list + group fields) and fixed artists-from-musicInfo. Code-reviewed (2 fixes applied), 54 tests pass, ruff/basedpyright/codespell clean.

title: [Epic] API surface completeness — remaining Gazelle ajax routes
description:
The Core API Client epic (python-gazelle-ahf, closed) shipped a deliberate read-only subset: index, torrent, browse, artist, request(s), collage, inbox, notifications, download (~11 actions, ~38% of the documented surface). This epic tracks the remaining documented ajax.php actions from the OPSnet fork (the codebase Orpheus and RED descend from; docs: https://github.com/OPSnet/Gazelle/blob/master/docs/07-API.md).

Missing READ actions: torrentgroup, user, usersearch, user_torrents, top10, bookmarks, subscriptions, forum (main/viewforum/viewthread), similar_artists, announcements, better, logchecker.

Missing WRITE actions: add_tag, add_log, request_fill/requestfill. (upload is owned by Cross-Upload epic python-gazelle-xn1, not duplicated here.)

Per-tracker caveat: action sets diverge slightly between Orpheus and RED; logchecker/better are RED-flavored tooling. Verify each against live fixtures from both trackers where possible.

title: Set up MkDocs + Material + mkdocstrings documentation site

close_reason: MkDocs+Material+mkdocstrings site implemented, builds clean in --strict, Pages deploy workflow added. Merged to main.

Fixtures are required for model tests (4.3/4.4) and will reveal tracker-specific schema divergences needed for task 4.2.
close_reason: Fixtures captured locally for both trackers (index, notifications, torrent, artist x2). Kept out of git by design (tests/fixtures/ gitignored — index responses contain passkey/authkey). capture_fixtures.py fixed for per-tracker auth + artist extraction. Divergences discovered and recorded on python-gazelle-6ue; the two failing Orpheus model tests are xfail pending 6ue.

title: Cross-seed: file list verification & .torrent generation
description: Compare local file lists against candidate release files to verify exact match, then produce a .torrent ready to add to a torrent client. Part of Cross-Seed epic (python-gazelle-7gj).

title: Cross-seed: API metadata matching
description: Query target tracker API by artist, album, format, year to find candidate releases for cross-seeding. Part of Cross-Seed epic (python-gazelle-7gj).

title: Upload monitoring callback/event model
description: Expose a callback/event interface so scripts can register handlers that fire on deletion or trump events. Part of Upload Monitoring epic (python-gazelle-p53).

title: Upload deletion/trump polling
description: Poll notifications and torrent endpoints to detect when user uploads are deleted or trumped. Part of Upload Monitoring epic (python-gazelle-p53).

title: [Epic] Cross-Seed
description: Find matching releases on a target tracker via API metadata match (artist, album, format, year) combined with local file list verification. Produces a .torrent ready to add to a torrent client. Depends on Core API Client epic.

title: [Epic] Upload Monitoring
description: Detect when user uploads are deleted or trumped on Orpheus/Redacted. Polls notifications and torrent endpoints, exposes a callback/event model so automation scripts can react (e.g., remove torrent from client). Depends on Core API Client epic.

title: browse: enrich BrowseTorrent + TorrentResult (per-edition stats, freeleech flags)
description:
Browse result models (action=browse) are very thin. Enrich:
- BrowseTorrent: add edition_id (editionId), artists, remaster_year/title/record_label/catalogue_number, has_log (hasLog), log_score (logScore), has_cue (hasCue), scene, vanity_house, time, per-edition snatches/seeders/leechers, and freeleech flags is_freeleech/is_neutral_leech/is_personal_freeleech/can_use_token/has_snatched.
- TorrentResult (group row): add cover, bookmarked, vanity_house, group_time (groupTime), release_type (string, e.g. "Album").
All Optional/tolerant. TDD against tests/fixtures/orpheus/browse.json + redacted/browse.json. Browse is a hot path.
close_reason: Enriched BrowseTorrent (edition_id, artists, remaster_*, has_log/log_score/has_cue, scene, vanity_house, time, seeders/leechers/snatches, freeleech flags) and TorrentResult (cover, bookmarked, vanity_house, release_type str, group_time raw). All Optional. Handled divergences (groupId str/int, releaseType str, groupTime datetime/epoch). TDD vs both fixtures. Reviewed clean. 111 tests pass, gate clean.

title: torrentgroup: add releaseTypeName (+ group-block extras)
description: TorrentGroup exposes release_type as an int code but drops releaseTypeName ("Album"/"EP"/"Single"...). Add release_type_name: str | None (alias releaseTypeName) so callers don't have to map the int themselves. Also consider group-block extras: proxy_image (proxyImage), wiki_bbcode (wikiBBcode), is_bookmarked (isBookmarked), time. Cheap win. TDD against tests/fixtures/orpheus/torrent.json (group block). Optional/tolerant.
close_reason: Added TorrentGroup.release_type_name (Orpheus-only), proxy_image (Orpheus-only), is_bookmarked, time, wiki_bbcode (AliasChoices wikiBBcode/bbBody — Orpheus vs RED key divergence). All Optional. TDD vs both fixtures. Reviewed clean. 106 tests pass, gate clean.

title: collage: expose contained torrent groups (torrentgroups list)
description:
Collage model currently exposes only id/name/description/tags/num_torrents and drops the actual torrent groups the collage contains. action=collage returns a "torrentgroups" array (each entry shaped like action=torrentgroup), which maps onto the existing TorrentGroup model.

Add: torrent_groups: list[TorrentGroup] = [] (explicit alias "torrentgroups" — to_camel would produce "torrentGroups", but the API sends all-lowercase "torrentgroups"). Reuse models.torrents.TorrentGroup; no new group model.

TDD: inline test validating a documented-shape collage payload parses its torrentgroups into TorrentGroup instances (with nested torrents), and that an absent key defaults to [].

CAVEAT: no collage fixture exists locally (tests/fixtures has only artist/browse/index/notifications/torrent). Modeled from the OPSnet API doc. Follow-up: capture a real collage fixture to verify the "torrentgroups" key + casing and to model the collage metadata scalars (creatorID, collageCategoryID/Name, subscriberCount, maxGroups, maxGroupsPerUser, locked, deleted, hasBookmarked, torrentGroupIDList, description_raw) whose casing can't be trusted from the doc summary. Ground truth: https://github.com/OPSnet/Gazelle/blob/master/docs/07-API.md
close_reason: Added Collage.torrent_groups (list[TorrentGroup], alias 'torrentgroups'), reusing TorrentGroup. action=collage's contained groups (+ their nested torrents) now parse. TDD inline (no collage fixture exists). Modeled from OPSnet API doc; follow-up to capture a fixture + model collage metadata scalars. Reviewed clean. 95 tests pass, gate clean.

title: user: surface freeleech tokens + bonus points in UserStats
description:
The index (me()) userstats block returns more than UserStats currently models; extra="ignore" silently drops them.

Orpheus userstats includes: tokens (freeleech token count), bonusPoints, bonusPointsPerHour, class — in addition to the already-modeled uploaded/downloaded/ratio/requiredratio. RED index userstats omits tokens/bonusPoints/bonusPointsPerHour (confirmed in tests/fixtures/redacted/index.json — block stops at class). Account owner also confirms RED surfaces no token count (they hold no tokens).

Add to UserStats as Optional (tracker-divergence convention — tolerant base model, no subclasses):
- tokens: int | None
- bonus_points: int | None (alias bonusPoints)
- bonus_points_per_hour: float | None (alias bonusPointsPerHour)
- user_class: str | None (alias "class" — Python keyword, needs explicit alias)

TDD: model test asserting Orpheus full block parses (tokens == 5 per fixture) and RED slimmer block still validates (tokens is None).

Follow-up (separate, optional): investigate whether RED exposes FL token count via a different endpoint (action=user profile or dedicated). Ground truth: https://github.com/OPSnet/Gazelle/blob/master/docs/07-API.md
close_reason: Added Optional tokens (freeleech token count), bonus_points, bonus_points_per_hour, user_class (alias 'class') to UserStats. Orpheus-only in index userstats; RED omits them so they stay None (tracker-divergence convention). TDD: fixture-driven Orpheus/RED tests + a fixture-free CI guard. Code-reviewed clean. 92 tests pass, ruff/basedpyright/codespell clean.

title: transport: write path (POST + auth token/CSRF + write-error mapping)
description: Foundational transport capability for all mutation endpoints. Today GazelleTransport.request() is GET-only and the only POST is _login() (cookie auth) — there is no generic write path. Add: a POST-based request method (form/multipart bodies), auth token / CSRF (auth_key) handling required by write actions, and write-specific error mapping. Per-tracker divergence likely (Orpheus vs RED token mechanics) — verify against both. This is a prerequisite shared by write endpoints (python-gazelle-5m0) and cross-upload submission (python-gazelle-xn1); neither should reimplement it. Ground truth: https://github.com/OPSnet/Gazelle/blob/master/docs/07-API.md
design:
SAFETY: writes are non-idempotent — do NOT reuse the GET retry policy.

The GET request() retries on 429/5xx (transport.py:115-123). For a write POST that is dangerous: a write that SUCCEEDED server-side but returned 5xx or timed out would be retried and double-apply (double add_tag, double request_fill). So the write path MUST NOT blind-retry non-idempotent POSTs:
- Default max_retries = 0 for writes, OR
- retry ONLY on errors where the request provably never landed (connection refused / pre-send failure), NEVER after the body may have been processed server-side.
This makes writes safe in production, not just in tests.

TESTING: build + test the entire write path against the mocked httpx client (the _http_client injection the read path and _login already use) — assert POST body, auth token, headers, error mapping, and the no-retry behavior. Zero live calls, zero ban risk. NO live integration tests that mutate tracker state (the downstream 5m0 actions — add_tag/add_log/request_fill — are not safely reversible). Any future live smoke test would be add_tag only, opt-in via env flag, single-shot, max_retries=0 — out of scope for 9l5.

SCOPE: 9l5 is transport plumbing only (generic request_write/POST + authkey/CSRF injection + write-error mapping); it calls no mutating action itself. authkey comes from the index/_login response (already parsed) — cache + inject as a POST param. Per-tracker token mechanics (Orpheus vs RED) verified against the OPSnet API doc.

title: write endpoints: add_tag, add_log, request_fill
description: Add mutation endpoints: add_tag (tag a torrent group), add_log (attach rip log to a torrent), request_fill/requestfill (mark request filled). Builds on the transport write path (python-gazelle-9l5, blocking prerequisite) — do NOT reimplement POST/CSRF handling here. NOTE: action=upload is intentionally excluded — owned by Cross-Upload epic python-gazelle-xn1.

title: forum: ForumResource (main, viewforum, viewthread)
description: New ForumResource covering the forum read actions: list categories (action=forum&type=main), threads in a forum (type=viewforum, paginated), and posts in a thread incl. polls (type=viewthread). New models for Category/Forum/Thread/Post. Larger than a single method — feature-sized.

title: site reads: top10 + announcements
description: Add site-level read endpoints. top10 (action=top10; ranked torrents/tags/users, param for type+limit) and announcements (action=announcements; site news/blog posts). New resource (e.g. SiteResource) + models.
close_reason: Implemented client.site (SiteResource.top10(type, limit) action=top10; announcements() action=announcements) + models (Top10Category, Announcements/Announcement/BlogPost), wired into async + sync clients. Code-reviewed: made Announcement.news_id/BlogPost.blog_id Optional per tracker-divergence convention. 89 tests pass, ruff/basedpyright/codespell clean.

title: artists: add similar() for similar_artists action
description: Add ArtistResource.similar(artist_id) calling ajax.php action=similar_artists, returning related artists. Small extension to the existing ArtistResource; reuse ArtistResult-style model.
close_reason: Implemented ArtistResource.similar() for action=similar_artists (+ SimilarArtist model). Code-reviewed (null-guard fix applied), 60 tests pass, lint clean.

title: personal lists: bookmarks + subscriptions
description: Add read access to the user's saved items. bookmarks (action=bookmarks, type=torrents|artists) and subscriptions (action=subscriptions, subscribed forum threads with unread status). New resource(s) + models. Both are auth'd, current-user endpoints.
close_reason: Added client.bookmarks (torrents/artists) and client.subscriptions (list) resources + models, wired into async + sync clients. Code-reviewed (release_type fix, missing fields added, _parse_list helper extracted; tag_list-as-string refuted). 79 tests pass, lint clean.

title: user: profile-by-id, usersearch, user_torrents
description: Extend UserResource beyond me()/index. Add: get(user_id) -> User (action=user, public profile + community stats); search(query) (action=usersearch); torrents(user_id, type) (action=user_torrents). Reuse/extend the User model; profile-by-id omits private fields present in index.
close_reason: Implemented UserResource.get()/search()/torrents() (actions user/usersearch/user_torrents) + models. Code-reviewed (Literal type, _params helper, field-name collision fix applied; several findings refuted with reasons). 68 tests pass, lint clean.

title: Fix pre-existing ruff lint + format violations repo-wide
description:
Running 'uv run ruff check .' reports 24 errors and 'uv run ruff format --check .' wants to reformat 9 files. These are pre-existing (predate python-gazelle-6ue) and live in files outside that change, so they were left untouched. This task clears them so the repo passes the lint/format gate documented in CLAUDE.md.

Lint (24 errors, all auto-fixable via 'ruff check --fix'):
  - 19x I001 (import block unsorted/unformatted)
  - 5x F401 (unused import)
Affected: src/pygazelle/__init__.py, client.py, resources/{artists,base,collages,inbox,notifications,requests,torrents,user}.py, tests/integration/{conftest,test_orpheus,test_redacted}.py, tests/models/conftest.py, tests/{test_client,test_errors,test_sync,test_transport}.py

Format (9 files via 'ruff format'): devtools/capture_fixtures.py, src/pygazelle/{__init__,client,sync}.py, src/pygazelle/models/__init__.py, src/pygazelle/resources/__init__.py, tests/{test_client,test_sync,test_transport}.py

Caution: before blindly applying F401 auto-fix, confirm the 5 unused imports are not intended public re-exports (check __all__ in any affected __init__.py). Run the full non-integration suite after fixing to confirm no regressions.
acceptance_criteria: 'uv run ruff check .' reports 0 errors; 'uv run ruff format --check .' reports all files formatted; 'uv run pytest --ignore=tests/integration' still passes (currently 42); no intended public re-export removed.

close_reason: Replaced template placeholder README with real library docs (usage, auth, resources, errors); examples verified against the public API.

Cannot be implemented until real API fixtures are captured and schema differences are known. Blocked on fixture capture task. Once fixtures are available, identify divergent fields and introduce tracker-specific submodels.
notes: Implemented on branch worktree-pygazelle-6ue-tracker-divergences (commit be39740). Made base Torrent/Artist tolerant rather than adding subclasses — see rationale. Model tests now 5 passed (was 3 passed + 2 xfailed). Quality gates pass for changed files (ruff, basedpyright 0 errors, codespell). NOT yet merged; pending code review + finish-branch.
close_reason: Implemented: base Torrent/Artist models made tolerant of Orpheus/RED divergences (commits be39740, 4b85d0b). Merged to main. Model tests 5 passed.

title: torrentgroup: expose collage memberships (collages + personalCollages)
description:
The action=torrent / action=torrentgroup group block carries the torrent group's collage memberships, which TorrentGroup currently drops (extra="ignore"). Observed in tests/fixtures/redacted/torrent.json group block:
- "collages": [{ "id", "name", "numTorrents" }]
- "personalCollages": [{ "id", "name", "numTorrents" }]

Add to TorrentGroup (Optional/tolerant): collages: list[Collage] and personal_collages: list[Collage] (alias personalCollages). The entries match the existing models.collages.Collage shape (id/name/num_torrents) — reuse it. NOTE potential import-direction concern: collages.py already imports torrents.TorrentGroup (for Collage.torrent_groups), so importing Collage into torrents.py would create a circular import. Resolve by either (a) a shared/light CollageRef model, (b) a forward ref + model_rebuild, or (c) moving the shared model. Decide during implementation.

Divergence: confirmed present on RED group block; verify Orpheus (the orpheus/torrent.json group block did not show collages — may be empty/absent there). Keep Optional. TDD against tests/fixtures/redacted/torrent.json. Discovered during python-gazelle-5gx (browse enrichment) / d5f. Ground truth: https://github.com/OPSnet/Gazelle/blob/master/docs/07-API.md

title: models: low-priority dropped fields (musicInfo credits, notifications meta, covers)
description:
Lower-priority dropped fields surfaced in the 2026-06 fixture audit (do as one cleanup task or split):
- Torrent group block musicInfo credits: with/remixedBy/composers/conductor/dj/producer/arranger (we only lift artists). Model as Optional credit lists.
- cover/proxyImage image URLs where not already added.
- Notifications pagination meta: action=notifications returns currentPage/pages/numNew alongside results; NotificationResource currently returns a bare list and drops them. Consider a paginated container or expose meta.
All Optional/tolerant. TDD against existing fixtures.

title: lint: exclude tests/fixtures from codespell
description: devtools/lint.py runs 'codespell src tests devtools', which scans locally-captured tests/fixtures/*.json (real tracker data, gitignored, regenerated per-dev). Real metadata trips codespell (e.g. 'Vai', 'NCE'), so 'make lint'/lint.py --check fails locally for anyone who has captured fixtures. CI is unaffected (fixtures absent). Fix: add skip for tests/fixtures in [tool.codespell] in pyproject.toml (e.g. skip = 'tests/fixtures'). Discovered while implementing python-gazelle-20t.
close_reason: Added skip='*/fixtures/*' to [tool.codespell]; codespell + lint.py --check now pass locally with captured fixtures present.

title: tooling reads: better + logchecker (RED-flavored)
description: Add the tooling read endpoints: better (action=better; single-seeded/transcode-candidate torrents) and logchecker (action=logchecker; validate ripping log quality). These are RED-flavored and may be absent/divergent on Orpheus — gate or mark Optional per the tracker-divergence convention. Lower priority/niche.

title: Commit sanitized fixtures so model tests run in CI
description: tests/fixtures/ is gitignored because real index.json responses contain passkey/authkey. As a result the model tests (tests/models/) skip in CI where no fixtures exist. Provide sanitized fixtures (redact passkey, authkey, announce URLs, and any account identifiers) that can be safely committed, so model parsing tests run in CI. Source data captured locally via devtools/capture_fixtures.py. Relates to python-gazelle-6ue (which fixes the model divergences those tests assert).

value: Fixture field-coverage audit (2026-06): models drop many response fields via extra="ignore". Tracked as beads under epic python-gazelle-mca. (1) Artist (j0u): drops discography "torrentgroup" [different shape: groupId/groupName/groupYear, tags as {id:name} map, singular "torrent" key, extendedArtists — needs own model, NOT TorrentGroup], statistics {numGroups,numTorrents,numSeeders,numLeechers,numSnatches,numRequests}, similarArtists {artistId,name,score,similarId}, plus vanityHouse/bodyBbcode/requests. (2) Torrent (zvi): drops remasterYear/Title/RecordLabel/CatalogueNumber, description, logChecksum/logCount/ripLogIds, freeReason, reported. (3) TorrentGroup (d5f): drops releaseTypeName (have int release_type only), proxyImage, wikiBBcode, isBookmarked, time. (4) Browse (5gx): BrowseTorrent very thin — drops editionId, artists, remaster*, hasLog/logScore/hasCue/scene, per-edition snatches/seeders/leechers, freeleech flags isFreeleech/isNeutralLeech/isPersonalFreeleech/canUseToken/hasSnatched; TorrentResult drops cover/bookmarked/vanityHouse/groupTime/releaseType(str). (5) low-pri (rfb): group musicInfo credits (with/remixedBy/composers/conductor/dj/producer/arranger), notifications pagination meta currentPage/pages/numNew. Local fixtures cover artist/browse/index/notifications/torrent only — NO collage/torrentgroup fixtures.

Gazelle ajax route coverage audit (2026-06): core API client (epic ahf, closed) implements ~11 of ~29 documented OPSnet-fork actions (~38%). IMPLEMENTED: index, torrent, browse, artist, request, requests, collage, inbox, inbox&viewconv, notifications, download. MISSING READ: torrentgroup, user, usersearch, user_torrents, top10, bookmarks, subscriptions, forum(main/viewforum/viewthread), similar_artists, announcements, better, logchecker. MISSING WRITE: add_tag, add_log, request_fill (upload owned by cross-upload epic xn1). Follow-ups tracked under epic python-gazelle-mca. Ground truth: https://github.com/OPSnet/Gazelle/blob/master/docs/07-API.md. Transport currently only does read GETs + download — write endpoints need a POST/CSRF path first.

Tracker schema divergences (Orpheus vs RED) are handled by making the BASE pydantic models tolerant (Optional fields + union types like 'bool | str | None'), NOT by per-tracker subclasses. Reason: resources like TorrentResource.get always build the base model regardless of tracker, and tests/models validate both trackers' fixtures against the same base class. Example (python-gazelle-6ue): Torrent.info_hash/remastered made Optional (Orpheus omits), Torrent.free_torrent is 'bool | str | None' (Orpheus sends 'Normal' string enum, RED sends bool), Artist.tags is 'list[ArtistTag] | list[str]' (Orpheus sends {name,count} objects). Subclasses would be dead code unless the resource layer dispatches per tracker.

Workflow preference: for small, well-specified endpoint-addition tasks under epic python-gazelle-mca (e.g. 20t get_group, f54 similar_artists), the user wants direct implementation — skip the formal /superpowers:brainstorming Q&A (they rejected the multiple-choice design question on 20t). Just: claim bd issue -> feature branch -> TDD (tests first) -> implement, making reasonable design calls consistent with existing patterns -> run full gate (pytest/ruff/basedpyright/codespell) -> /code-review -> finishing-a-development-branch (merge to main locally + push). State design decisions briefly in chat rather than asking. Heavy Workflow 2 ceremony (opsx delta spec, writing-plans) is NOT wanted for these one-endpoint tasks.
